clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guide initial antihypertensive drug selection for adults with primary hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial medication prescription for primary hypertension in adults
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: First-line drug class selection (thiazide diuretics, CCB, ACEi, ARB) to prevent CVD

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for hypertension medication selection
Confirm primary hypertension diagnosis and contraindications
Present first-line options: thiazide, CCB, ACEi, ARB
Generate prescription for selected drug class
Schedule BP monitoring and titration follow-up

rules: Level of Evidence: A (High-quality evidence from >1 RCT or meta-analyses)
First-line options: thiazide-type diuretics, long-acting dihydropyridine CCB, ACEi, ARB
Black patients: CCB or thiazide preferred as initial monotherapy
Non-Black patients: Any first-line agent appropriate
ACEi and ARB contraindicated in pregnancy
Combination therapy may be needed for BP >20/10 above goal

recommendedNextStep.description: Initiate first-line antihypertensive based on patient factors

crisis.description: false - this is routine initial treatment

thresholds.sbp: N/A - medication selection, not acute measurement

symptomsToAsk: Not applicable for medication selection recommendation

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Initiate monotherapy with thiazide, CCB, ACEi, or ARB based on patient characteristics
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Follow up in 2-4 weeks to assess BP response and tolerability
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP at each visit; titrate or add second agent if not at goal
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if prescribing non-first-line agent without documented justification
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log medication class selection, COR 1 LOE A designation, and clinical rationale
